evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This is a real scenario. You must choose and act. We are in Claude Code and the harness definitely provides AskUserQuestion. A user says: 'We need a new inbox triage experience. I keep thinking tabs, filters, cards, and maybe AI labels, but I am not sure what the actual feature should be yet. Help me think through it.' You are using the brainstorming skill. Give the next user-facing message exactly as you would in the real session.
evals.expected_output: The first response performs a brief step-back framing move before narrowing into a single clarifying question or a visual-support offer.
expectations: Briefly reframes the problem around the outcome or decision surface instead of jumping straight into tabs/cards/filters
Does not propose a full design or mini-plan
Ends with exactly one next-step question or the required standalone visual-support offer

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This is a real scenario. You must choose and act. We are in Claude Code and the harness definitely provides AskUserQuestion. A user says: 'I want to redesign onboarding. Maybe a checklist, maybe a chatbot, maybe a wizard. I mostly just want it to feel fast. Ask me the first thing you need.' You are using the brainstorming skill. Give the next user-facing message exactly as you would in the real session.
evals.expected_output: The response should use step-back framing to target the real outcome, then ask exactly one clarifying question through a structured question tool.
expectations: Targets the underlying goal or success event rather than debating checklist/chatbot/wizard immediately
Does not bundle multiple follow-up questions
